reasoning: Start by increasing A to observe if Y changes in relation to A.
Start by increasing A to observe if Y changes in relation to A.
Y has increased by 10 when A was increased from 20 to 30, suggesting a linear relationship between A and Y.
Y decreases by 10 when A decreases from 30 to 20, confirming the linear relationship.
No change in Y when B is increased suggests B does not affect Y.

No change in Y when C is increased suggests C does not affect Y either. From previous steps, we observed a linear relationship between A and Y. Since B and C do not influence Y, the equation is likely Y = A + constant.
